all_references.why_relevant: Cited by both US seeds; discusses FPV deployment using mooring buoys and a tensioning frame surrounding PV array—useful adjacent mooring-array context though not specific to concrete block/cross-float retention.

Cited by both US seeds; floating PV platform with multiple floats + primary frame + module frame; includes a broad list of anchoring means and adjustable positioning—potentially relevant to float/frame architecture and mooring concepts.

Cited by both US seeds; floating solar stage device with outer frame portion and central connecting rod plus multiple buoyant collars—general floating stage structure but not close to cross-pontoon retention or monitoring.

Cited by both US seeds; uses a construction grid of beams/crossbeams with off-the-shelf flotation elements tied underneath and anchoring via geogrid—relevant for alternative float-retention vocabulary (grid + ties).

Cited by both US seeds; FPV platform with pontoon bodies in groups fixed to a metal frame; adjustable tilt PV modules with actuator arms; two opposite PV fields separated by service path—helpful for mounting-structure art and pontoon grouping.
Cites triangular FPV mount seed; floating PV bracket with rotating beam and tracking mechanism—adjacent mounting/bracket vocabulary but likely not bifacial-specific.

Cites triangular FPV mount seed; modular bracket/base units for on-water PV with angled supporting rods—relevant to FPV module support/bracket structures (triangular/angled rods) though bifacial not explicit.

final_coverage.notes: No clear rectangular divided deck/walls disclosure from citations fetched.
No cruciform internal 4-opening pontoon retention found in citation set.
Some additional FPV bracket/platform mounting art; still not clearly bifacial + triangular supports.
Citations mention mooring generally but not concrete block + coated SS corner bridle.
No dual-side temperature + irradiance telemetry disclosure in citations.
No clear junction-box I/V telemetry disclosure in citations.
